vulnerability.title: Add nodev Option to /dev/shm
vulnerability.description: The nodev mount option can be used to prevent creation of device files in /dev/shm. Legitimate character and block devices should not exist within temporary directories like /dev/shm. Add the nodev option to the fourth column of /etc/fstab for the line which controls mounting of /dev/shm.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if ( ! ( { rpm --quiet -q kernel-core ;} && { rpm --quiet -q rpm-ostree ;} && { rpm --quiet -q bootc ;} && { ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet ;} && ([ -f /run/ostree-booted ] || [ -L /ostree ]) ) && ! ( [ -f /.dockerenv ] || [ -f /run/.containerenv ] ) ); then function perform_remediation { mount_point_match_regexp="$(printf "^[[:space:]]*[^#].*[[:space:]]%s[[:space:]]" /dev/shm)" # If the mount point is not in /etc/fstab, get previous mount options from /etc/mtab if ! grep -q "$mount_point_match_regexp" /etc/fstab; then # runtime opts without some automatic kernel/userspace-added defaults previous_mount_opts=$(grep "$mount_point_match_regexp" /etc/mtab | head -1 | awk '{print $4}' \ | sed -E "s/(rw|defaults|seclabel|nodev)(,|$)//g;s/

triage_decision.reason: Adding the nodev option to /dev/shm prevents device files from being created in a temporary directory, a standard hardening practice that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services.

execution_details.stdout:
#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults,nodev        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults 0 0

#
# /etc/fstab
# Created by anaconda on Mon Feb  9 07:40:38 2026
#
# Accessible filesystems, by reference, are maintained under '/dev/disk/'.
# See man pages fstab(5), findfs(8), mount(8) and/or blkid(8) for more info.
#
# After editing this file, run 'systemctl daemon-reload' to update systemd
# units generated from this file.
#
/dev/mapper/rlm-root    /                       xfs     defaults        0 0
UUID=8bbec118-7baf-40c6-910b-6a336e901242 /boot xfs     defaults,nodev        0 0
UUID=F56C-228D          /boot/efi               vfat    umask=0077,shortname=winnt 0 2
/dev/mapper/rlm-home    /home                   xfs     defaults        0 0
/dev/mapper/rlm-swap    none                    swap    defaults        0 0
tmpfs /dev/shm tmpfs defaults 0 0

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan's intent—to add the nodev option to the /dev/shm entry in /etc/fstab and remount—is sound and would mitigate the vulnerability.  The script appears to handle platform checks, locate the fstab entry, and modify mount options, though the provided snippet is truncated and does not show the complete logic or error handling.  If executed fully and correctly, it should resolve the issue.  The implementation could be refined to improve reliability and safety.
concerns: Script is incomplete in the provided excerpt, so correctness of all edge cases cannot be confirmed.
It appears to manipulate /etc/fstab directly without creating a backup, which could be risky if something goes wrong.
Use of /etc/mtab for fallback may not reflect the authoritative mount configuration on modern systems that use systemd.
suggested_improvements:
Add a command to back up /etc/fstab before modifying it. 
Ensure the script checks whether nodev is already present to avoid duplicate options or unintended option removal. 
Use the `mount` command with `--make-rprivate` or `remount` to apply changes immediately after fstab update. 
Consider using `systemctl daemon-reload` or `systemd-escape` if systemd unit files are involved for /dev/shm. 
Run validation tests in a staging environment to confirm that the script preserves any other relevant mount options such as `noexec` or `nosuid`.
Include robust error handling and exit status checks after each critical operation (e.g., editing fstab, unmounting, remounting).
